output_summary: aligned schemas for datasets: amazon_small, goodreads_small, metabooks_small; generated 2 schema correspondence groups.
profiled datasets: amazon_small, goodreads_small, metabooks_small.
Reused the blocking configuration from state.
Blocking uses `semantic_similarity` for goodreads_small_amazon_small, metabooks_small_amazon_small, metabooks_small_goodreads_small, blocking on title, author, publish_year.
Performance-wise, pair completeness ranges from 0.99 to 1.00 and candidate counts range from 149304 to 149916.
The config also records dataset id columns.

matcher mode `ml` with config source reused from state; evaluated pairs: id_columns, matching_strategies; selected scores: matching_strategies (threshold=0.78, f1=0.9080459770114943, threshold=0.75, f1=0.906367041198502, threshold=0.7); produced 2 pair strategy entries.
This run was an initial draft of the pipeline.

The pipeline was constructed to include schema matching using an LLM-based matcher, embedding-based blocking with a sentence-transformer model, and ML-based entity matching with feature extraction and model selection via GridSearchCV.
A data fusion strategy was added that uses the 'longest_string' fuser for 11 attributes and the 'union' fuser for the 'genres' attribute.
The fusion behavior is dominated by the 'longest_string' strategy for most attributes, with 'union' used specifically for the 'genres' attribute.
Singleton records are excluded from the fusion process.
The pipeline supports embedding-based blocking, CSV loading, and environment variable loading.
Schema matching aligns the schemas of 'goodreads_small' and 'metabooks_small' datasets to 'amazon_small' using an LLM-based matcher.
Blocking uses an embedding blocker with a pre-trained sentence-transformer model and top-k nearest neighbors.
Feature extraction uses string, numeric, and date comparators with specific similarity functions and thresholds tailored per dataset pair.
pipeline execution succeeded on attempt 1; fusion_data.csv was produced.

Added extensive schema matching, blocking, ML-based matching, clustering, and data fusion code in pipeline.py; integrated ChatOpenAI LLM for schema matching; defined embedding blockers with sentence-transformers model; configured multiple comparators for three dataset pairs; loaded ground truth CSVs; extracted features; performed GridSearchCV over RandomForest, LogisticRegression, GradientBoosting, and SVM classifiers; selected best models; matched entities with MLBasedMatcher.
This run was an initial draft of the evaluation adaption.
The dominant evaluation strategy used was a data fusion evaluation strategy named 'ml_fusion_strategy_books' that applies multiple attribute-specific evaluation functions.
Core attributes such as title, author, and publisher were evaluated using tokenized matching.
Numeric fields like publish_year, page_count, and price were evaluated using numeric tolerance matching.
List-like fields such as genres and any additional detected list-like columns were evaluated using set equality matching.
Language was evaluated using tokenized matching if present.
Added helper functions to parse source IDs and detect gold ID prefixes for alignment.
Implemented logic to build an evaluation view aligning fused data IDs with gold standard IDs.
Included detection and normalization of list-like columns across fused and gold datasets.
Added support for loading gold test sets from XML or CSV formats.
Configured evaluation functions per attribute and registered them with the evaluation strategy.
Enabled debug output to a JSONL file for detailed evaluation traceability.
Persisted evaluation results to a JSON file for further analysis.
evaluation execution succeeded on attempt 1; pipeline_evaluation.json is available.
overall accuracy is 58.57%; evaluation attempt counter is 1.
The dominant problem in this run is poor fusion quality causing low accuracy in key attributes, especially genres, author, and title.
There are systematic missing fused values for some gold IDs due to fusion dropping singletons and incomplete ID mapping in evaluation.
Fusion uses inappropriate longest_string fusers for normalized attributes, causing semantic mismatches with gold data.
Genres fusion produces lists of lists of genre strings instead of a single flattened set, causing near-total failure on genres accuracy.
Numeric attributes like page_count are fused as strings without numeric aggregation, causing mismatches despite tolerant evaluation.
Blocking and matching perform well and are not the main source of errors.
Overall accuracy is 0.586 across 140 attribute checks on 20 gold records.
Genres accuracy is only 0.05 with 19/20 records wrong due to set mismatches.
Author accuracy is 0.45 and title accuracy is 0.60, with errors mainly from extra decorations or series info in fused values.
Publisher and page count accuracies are moderate at 0.65, with publisher mismatches due to extra imprint words and page count differences of 10-20 pages.
This run was an evaluation refinement of the existing pipeline.

The pipeline currently uses the 'longest_string' fusion strategy for 11 attributes including title, author, publisher, language, bookformat, edition, publish_year, rating, numratings, page_count, and price.
The 'union' fusion strategy is used for the genres attribute.
Singleton retention is disabled (include_singletons=False), meaning entities that do not match others are dropped from the fusion output.
There was no change in fusion or matching behavior in this run; the pipeline maintained the same fusion strategies and matching configurations.
The fusion strategy continues to prefer the longest string for most attributes, which leads to verbose variants being selected.
The union strategy for genres does not flatten nested lists, causing mismatches with the evaluation's expected flattened set.
The pipeline includes embedding-based blocking support and CSV and environment loading capabilities.
No changes were made to attribute fusers or thresholds in this run.
The pipeline does not include singletons in the fusion output, which causes some gold IDs to be missing in the fused results.
pipeline execution succeeded on attempt 1; fusion_data.csv was produced.

Removed unused numpy import in pipeline.py; clarified comments on id column alignment and schema matching capitalization; replaced semantic_similarity blocking with EmbeddingBlocker using top_k=15; added import and fallback sys.path logic for list_normalization in evaluation.py; implemented build_eval_view function to align fused and gold IDs with prefix detection and source ID parsing; added list-like column detection and normalization.
This run was an evaluation refinement.
The dominant evaluation strategy used was tokenized_match for text fields, set_equality_match for list-like fields, numeric_tolerance_match for numeric fields, and year_only_match for publish_year.
Genres showed very low accuracy due to fusion producing lists of lists instead of a flattened set, causing set equality mismatches.
Author and Title had moderate accuracy with errors mainly from missing fused values for some gold IDs and mismatches due to extra decorations or series info in fused values.
Publisher and Page Count had moderate accuracy with mismatches caused by semantic but non-identical strings and numeric differences respectively.
Language and Publish Year had good accuracy with remaining errors mostly due to missing fused rows for some gold IDs.
Changed gold data loading to prefer CSV over XML if CSV exists to match provided data format.
Updated evaluation strategy to use year_only_match for publish_year instead of numeric_tolerance_match.
Added conditional registration of evaluation functions only if attributes exist in both fused and gold datasets.
Improved evaluation coverage by refining the build_eval_view function to better map fused records to gold IDs.
evaluation execution succeeded on attempt 1; pipeline_evaluation.json is available.
overall accuracy is 60.00%; previous logged accuracy was 58.57%; evaluation attempt counter is 2.
The main problem in this run is that the data fusion output has low accuracy due to representation mismatches, poor normalization, and coverage gaps.
Specifically, the genres attribute has catastrophic accuracy loss because fused genres are lists of comma-separated strings rather than sets of atomic tags as in the gold data.
Authors, titles, and publishers have systematic formatting and normalization errors causing mismatches.
Additionally, some gold entities are missing from the fused output entirely due to exclusion of singletons in fusion.
Fusion logic uses longest_string selection for many attributes, which favors verbose noisy values instead of canonical forms.
Numeric attributes are fused as strings, causing inconsistent and inaccurate values.
Genres accuracy is only 0.05 with all 20 debug lines showing mismatches due to fused genres being 2-element lists of comma-separated strings versus gold's flattened list of tags.
Author accuracy is 0.45 with mismatches caused by extra role annotations like '(Goodreads Author)' and multiple contributors.
Title and publisher accuracies are 0.65 with errors from extra series/edition info and imprint/HTML entity differences.
This run was an initial draft of the pipeline.
The fusion strategy was defined with 12 attribute fusers: 11 attributes use the 'longest_string' fuser and 'genres' uses the 'union' fuser.
Singletons are excluded from the fusion output by setting 'include_singletons' to False.
Fusion behavior favors selecting the longest string value for most attributes, which tends to select more verbose and noisy values rather than normalized or canonical forms.
The 'genres' attribute fusion uses a union operation on raw comma-separated strings, resulting in a list of large strings rather than atomic genre tags.
No changes were made to matching thresholds or blocking configurations.
The pipeline uses embedding-based blocking and ML-based matching with precomputed comparators and feature extractors.
Evaluation revealed systematic errors due to representation mismatches, source selection biases, and missing fused values caused by excluding singletons.
Using 'longest_string' fusion for most attributes simplifies fusion but leads to systematic mismatches with gold data due to noisy and verbose source values.
pipeline execution succeeded on attempt 1; fusion_data.csv was produced.

evaluation.py updated to prefer CSV gold test set if present, else XML; evaluation registration now conditional on attribute presence in both fused and gold sets; publish_year evaluation changed from numeric_tolerance_match to year_only_match; added evaluation for rating, numratings, bookformat, edition if present; improved print formatting of evaluation metrics.
This run was an evaluation refinement.

The dominant evaluation strategy used was set equality matching for genres, tokenized matching for author, title, publisher, and language, year-only matching for publish_year, and numeric tolerance matching for page_count and other numeric attributes.
Genres exhibited catastrophic accuracy issues due to representation mismatches between fused and gold data.
Authors showed systematic but mild formatting errors related to fusion and normalization.
Titles had errors mostly from extra series or edition information causing mismatches.
Publishers had imprint and formatted name differences causing mismatches.
Language, publish_year, and page_count performed relatively well with higher accuracy.
Missing fused values caused false negatives due to exclusion of singletons in fusion.
The evaluation code was refined to better align fused IDs to gold IDs.
List-like columns were detected and normalized across fused and gold datasets.
Evaluation functions were registered per attribute using appropriate matching functions.
Coverage diagnostics were added to measure direct and mapped ID coverage.
The evaluation strategy was corrected and evaluation metrics were persisted and printed in a structured format.
evaluation execution succeeded on attempt 1; pipeline_evaluation.json is available.
overall accuracy is 55.71%; previous logged accuracy was 60.00%; evaluation attempt counter is 3.

Added extensive new code in pipeline.py implementing schema matching with LLMBasedSchemaMatcher using ChatOpenAI GPT-5.1; blocking with EmbeddingBlocker on three datasets; feature extraction with multiple comparators; loading ground truth; training ML models with GridSearchCV over RandomForest, LogisticRegression, GradientBoosting, and SVM; ML-based matching and clustering with MaximumBipartiteMatching; saving correspondences; data fusion with DataFusionStrategy and DataFusionEngine.
